input.command:
kubectl get pods -n mongodb -o json | python3 -c "
import json,sys
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
for p in data['items']:
    name = p['metadata']['name']
    labels = p['metadata'].get('labels', {})
    conditions = p['status'].get('conditions', [])
    ready_cond = [c for c in conditions if c['type'] == 'Ready']
    print(f'{name}: labels={labels}')
    print(f'  Ready condition: {ready_cond}')
"
input.description: Check pod labels and Ready conditions

input.command: kubectl get configmap -n mongodb health-check-script -o yaml 2>&1
input.description: Check health-check-script configmap

content.content:
apiVersion: v1
data:
  health-check.sh: |
    #!/bin/sh
    set -e
    PW_FILE="/etc/health/health-password"
    OVERRIDE="/etc/health/overrides/${POD_NAME}"
    if [ -f "$OVERRIDE" ]; then
      HEALTH_PW="$(cat "$OVERRIDE")"
    else
      HEALTH_PW="$(cat "$PW_FILE")"
    fi
    mongosh --quiet "mongodb://health-user:${HEALTH_PW}@localhost:27017/admin" --eval 'db.hello().ok' >/dev/null
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |

input.command: kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- cat /etc/health/health-password 2>&1 && kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- cat /usr/local/bin/health-check.sh 2>&1
input.description: Check health password and health check script

content.content:
healthpass123#!/bin/sh
set -e
PW_FILE="/etc/health/health-password"
OVERRIDE="/etc/health/overrides/${POD_NAME}"
if [ -f "$OVERRIDE" ]; then
  HEALTH_PW="$(cat "$OVERRIDE")"
else
  HEALTH_PW="$(cat "$PW_FILE")"
fi
mongosh --quiet "mongodb://health-user:${HEALTH_PW}@localhost:27017/admin" --eval 'db.hello().ok' >/dev/null

content.thinking:
Good, the replica set is actually in a good state:
- mongodb-replica-0: SECONDARY
- mongodb-replica-1: PRIMARY
- mongodb-replica-2: SECONDARY

All pods are Ready. The issue is the `monitoring` label is missing from the pod template.

Let me check what the task requires:
1. Restore a stable 3-member replica set without data loss - ✅ Already working
2. All pods must become Ready - ✅ Already Ready
3. Replica set should have 1 PRIMARY and 2 SECONDARY members - ✅ Already the case
4. Restore the pod-template `monitoring` label to `enabled` so monitoring can see the members - Need to patch the StatefulSet

I need to patch the StatefulSet to add the missing `monitoring: enabled` label to the pod template, which will trigger a rolling update of the pods.

content.text: The replica set is healthy (1 PRIMARY + 2 SECONDARY, all pods Ready). The only issue is the missing `monitoring: enabled` label on the pod template. I need to patch the StatefulSet to add it.

input.command: kubectl patch statefulset mongodb-replica -n mongodb --type='json' -p='[{"op":"add","path":"/spec/template/metadata/labels/monitoring","value":"enabled"}]' 2>&1
input.description: Add monitoring=enabled label to pod template

content.text: All pods are Ready. Now let me verify the replica set topology and labels.

input.command:
kubectl get pods -n mongodb -o json | python3 -c "
import json,sys
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
for p in data['items']:
    name = p['metadata']['name']
    labels = p['metadata'].get('labels', {})
    ready = any(c['status']=='True' for c in p['status'].get('conditions',[]) if c['type']=='Ready')
    print(f'{name}: Ready={ready}, monitoring={labels.get(\"monitoring\",\"MISSING\")}, labels={labels}')
"
input.description: Verify pod labels and Ready status

content.thinking:
mongodb-replica-0 still has the old revision hash and is missing the `monitoring` label — the rolling update started from replica-2 downward (2 → 1 → 0). Replica-0 hasn't been updated yet. The StatefulSet rolling update for StatefulSets with `podManagementPolicy: Parallel` still updates pods in reverse ordinal order (2, 1, 0). So replica-0 hasn't been rolled yet.

The server quiesce mode error for the mongosh connection is just because the rolling update is still in progress (mongodb-replica-1 was just restarted, there's an election happening).
I'll wait for the rollout to complete before checking the status again.

content.text: The rolling update is still progressing — replica-0 hasn't been updated yet. Let me wait for the full rollout.

input.command: kubectl rollout status statefulset/mongodb-replica -n mongodb 2>&1 && kubectl get pods -n mongodb 2>&1
